mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand side of the economy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the federal funds rate above 5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in payroll employment and decrease in unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of strengthening labor market are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply side of the economy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods of increasing industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the real personal consumption expenditures above $370 billion signals an increase in consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing consumer spending are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the consumer price index signals a decrease in inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of decreasing inflation expectations are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in payroll employment and decrease in unemployment rate, accompanied by an increase in industrial production, signals a strengthening labor market and supply side, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods of strengthening labor market and supply side are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP signals an increase in economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of increasing economic growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
